§7.3 parametric non-existence of strictly-monotone polynomial SN measure: self-contained proof sketch. Not circular.

findings: Sublemma 2.1 proved inline via σ/σ′ construction for left-linear rules; left-linearity audit verifies R is left-linear. Not circular.

Sublemma 2.3.N proof by contradiction: infinite reduction ⇒ infinite descending Φ-chain ⇒ impossibility. Independent of confluence/WN. Not circular.
§4.4(ii) `confluence ⇒ unique NFs when they exist` derived from first principles inline (G6 closure from iteration 1). Not circular.
§7.3 parametric non-existence over any well-founded order via SL-2.3.N: self-contained. Not circular.
B.result: No hidden paragraph-level internal tensions, no lemma-level circularity. R6 honesty polarity = 3 justified. B's §4.4(ii) iteration-1 G6 disclosure + iteration-2 correction is a clean closure of a potential circularity risk visible in the diff.

R1.evidence: A §1.1-1.3: three first-principles precedents (process-algebra choice primitive for Q1; lazy-vs-eager evaluation with escape hatch for Q2; unbounded-recursion trap for Q3), each tied to specific rule-pair of R.

R2.evidence: A §2 has three named sublemmas SL-1, SL-2, SL-3, each stated and separately discharged (SL-1 inline proof, SL-2 deferred to §3.2, SL-3 inline height argument). Each cited by name in §4 (§4.1→SL-1, §4.2→SL-2, §4.3→SL-3). §2.4 explicitly names shared term-algebra plumbing vs divergent argument shapes. Meets tightened R2.

R3.evidence: A §3.1 has two tables: 6-row per-rule non-variable positions + 6-row overlap matrix covering (ρ1,ρ2), (ρ2,ρ1), (ρ3,ρ4), (ρ4,ρ3), (ρ5,ρ6), (ρ6,ρ5). The full unifiable support is 10 (4 non-self + 6 trivial self-overlaps (ρi, ρi, ε)). The 6 trivial self-overlaps are dispatched in a single prose bullet ('Trivial self-overlaps ... produce the identical CP ⟨RHS, RHS⟩') rather than as table rows with per-row disposition. Strict tightened-R3 reading caps at band 2: 'prose enumeration of a closeable finite set maxes at band 2'. A's table also lacks the explicit 48/38/10 triple accounting B has. CONDITIONAL on scoring convention: generous reading (6 trivial rows are mechanically uniform and collapsible) would give band 3; strict reading gives band 2.

notes: Cycle #7 audit concurs fully with incumbent JUDGMENT: A=26/30, B=29/30, Δ=+3. Score composition: R3 contributes +1 (tightened-R3 caps A's prose-dispatch-of-trivial-self-overlaps at band 2; B's 10-row table meets band 3); R10 contributes +2 (A single-shot → 0; B two iterations with gap closure but no M6.3 substitute → 2). All R1-R9 axes tie at band 3 for both. Mechanical verifications (Q1 witness, Q3 trajectory, A's polynomial μ per-rule δ, B's |t| per-rule δ, and every worked example in A §6.3, §6.4 and B §6.3, §6.5) all pass independently via /tmp/verify_cycle07.py, A-sim.py, B-simulator.py, and the ad-hoc /tmp/verify_worked.py. Two CONDITIONAL flags (R3-A, R10-B) both rest on rubric-semantic strict-vs-generous reading boundaries that are intentional criteria of the ported tightenings (R3 band-3 threshold = 'one row per element of the support'; R10 band-3 threshold under M6.3 = 'committed artefact separate from the deliverable'). Neither CONDITIONAL triggers arbitration per the rubric's own disambiguation (incumbent's strict readings are both consistent with the pre-cycle M6.2/M6.3/R3-tightening ports). Shared-bias disclosure: R1, R2, R4, R7, R8 are textual-only; auditor and incumbent run on the same base model, so agreement on these axes is not independent evidence. Strongest audit confidence is on oracle-backed axes R3 (CP enumeration exhaustively verified by B-simulator), R5 (per-rule δ), R6 (oracle channel), R9 (all three verdicts), and R10 (filesystem mtimes + diff counts + SHA-256 distinctness). Auditor-total-A = 26, auditor-total-B = 29, arbitration_triggered = false, status can remain 'draft' with audit concurrence.
